138.reason: The skipChar method has a logic error when moving to the next line. The calculation of posWord after moving to the next line is incorrect, causing the position to be off by one or more characters. This leads to incorrect tokenization, especially after multi-line comments or when tokens span lines.

186.reason: // Error: incorrect calculation for remaining length when crossing line boundaries; should use `len - (cnt - posWord)` instead

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: The calculation of remaining length is incorrect. When moving to the next line, `len` should be reduced by the characters consumed on the current line, but the formula `cnt - (getLine(posLine).length() - 1 - posWord)` is wrong. It should be `len - (cnt - posWord)`. This causes incorrect position tracking, leading to the extra "DIV /" token at the end of the output.]

134.reason: When skipping the closing '*/' of a comment at the end of the file, this logic sets posWord to the index of the last character ('/'). Consequently, the main loop processes this '/' as a DIV token instead of ignoring it as part of the comment. It should set posWord to getLine(posLine).length() to indicate the line has been fully consumed.
